evals.prompt: Our PDP for our flagship daily greens powder is converting at 1.2%. Industry benchmark is 2-3%. What's broken?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis grounded in the value-prop, message-match, and friction frameworks. Should run the 5-second value-prop test first, then check message-match between paid traffic creatives and PDP H1, then walk the PDP-specific friction taxonomy. Should pull device-split conversion before recommending fixes. Should not lead with button-color or theme changes.
assertions: Checks for .agents/modern-ai-context.md
Runs the 5-second value-prop test as the first diagnostic
Calls modern.attribution.first_touch grouped by landing_page
Calls modern.sales.aov segmented by traffic_source and device
Walks PDP-specific friction taxonomy (images, variant selector, trust signals)
Asks about mobile vs desktop conversion split
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
Names a single highest-leverage next move

evals.prompt: Cart conversion rate dropped from 68% to 54% over the past month. Nothing else changed.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis that walks the cart-specific friction taxonomy: shipping reveal, payment options, account-required friction, trust signals at cart. Should pull traffic-source and device split to check whether mix shifted. Should reference cart-abandonment-recovery for the deeper recovery-flow design. Should not assume the cart itself broke without checking the upstream mix.
assertions: Pulls device split and traffic source split before assuming the cart broke
Walks cart-specific friction taxonomy
References cart-abandonment-recovery for recovery-flow design
Checks whether channel mix shifted in the past month
Asks about any pricing or shipping changes in the window
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Mobile conversion is 0.8%. Desktop is 2.4%. Why?
evals.expected_output: Activates the mobile-first CRO framework. Should note that a 3x gap (200% gap, far above the 25-40% healthy range) indicates the page is desktop-designed and mobile-shrunk. Should walk the mobile-first checklist: 5-second value prop on mobile, sticky-bottom add-to-cart, single-column forms, tap target size, wallet payment availability. Should recommend fixing the mobile experience before any other CRO work.
assertions: Identifies the 3x gap as severe (well above 25-40% healthy band)
Walks the mobile-first CRO framework
Recommends sticky-bottom add-to-cart as a Quick Win
Checks Apple Pay / Google Pay availability and tap path
Recommends fixing mobile before any desktop-focused CRO
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We're running Meta ads promoting a 30-day skincare reset, and the landing page is just our homepage. Conversion is bad. Help.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses message-match failure between ad creative and landing page. Should walk the message-match framework, recommend building a campaign-specific landing page with the H1 echoing the ad headline. Should reference ad-creative for the upstream creative-traffic pairing question and programmatic-seo when many ad variants need many pages.
assertions: Identifies the issue as message-match failure
Walks the message-match framework
Recommends a campaign-specific landing page with H1 echoing ad headline
References ad-creative for upstream creative-traffic pairing
Does not recommend changing the homepage to match every ad
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We're redesigning our entire PDP. What should we test first?
evals.expected_output: Pushback against the 'redesign everything at once' approach because it isolates nothing. Should recommend testing the value-prop hierarchy, then message-match, then trust signal placement, then friction taxonomy in order. Should reference ab-testing for sample-size discipline. Should warn that a redesign with no holdout will not reveal which change drove the result.
assertions: Pushes back against a full redesign with no isolation
Recommends a sequence of isolated tests starting with value-prop
References ab-testing for sample-size discipline
Warns against changing everything at once
Recommends a redesign-vs-control holdout if a full redesign ships
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our PDP has 5 CTAs above the fold: add to cart, subscribe and save, find my shade quiz, free shipping ticker, and join rewards. People aren't converting.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses CTA-hierarchy failure. Walks the one-primary-CTA framework. Recommends elevating one CTA (likely add-to-cart or subscribe-and-save depending on subscription mix) and visually subordinating the rest. Should not recommend removing the secondary modules entirely — they have value, just not at primary-CTA visual weight.
assertions: Identifies CTA-hierarchy failure as the dominant leak
Walks the one-primary-CTA framework
Recommends elevating one CTA and subordinating others
Does not recommend deleting all secondary modules
Considers subscription-vs-one-time mix in choosing the primary CTA
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our supplement PDP says 'Maximum Bioavailability Daily Greens Powder Complex.' Should I change it?
evals.expected_output: Yes — the H1 fails the 5-second test for first-time visitors who don't know the product. Should recommend rewriting the H1 to lead with category + outcome + one trust signal. Provides a worked example. References copywriting for the deeper headline-as-promise framing.
assertions: Identifies that the H1 fails the 5-second test
Recommends rewriting H1 to lead with category + outcome + trust signal
Provides a worked rewrite example
References copywriting for deeper headline framing
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
